designConstraints: Modal overlay panel only — no full-page navigation, NavigationTabs, TopBar, or sidebar included
Overlay background uses rgba(0,0,0,0.65) per overlay semantic token to dim the dashboard beneath
Modal panel background uses navy-raised (#293548) at elevation level 3 (0 12px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5))
Border radius large (16px) applied to modal panel container
Z-index 1200 (modal layer) to sit above sticky elements and dropdowns
Maximum modal width constrained to ~400px centered in viewport for focused destructive confirmation pattern
Single visual hierarchy level: title, supporting body text, then two action buttons — no nested cards or sub-sections
Destructive action (Confirm Delete) uses coral-error (#F87171) as primary button color, NOT gold-amber CTA, to signal danger
Cancel action uses surface-elevated (navy-raised) background with slate-border (#334155) border as a ghost/secondary button
Close/dismiss X button required in top-right corner of modal panel per overlay design rules
Minimum touch target 44x44px for all interactive elements (X button, Cancel, Confirm Delete)
Typography: heading-2 (DM Sans, 16px, weight 600) for modal title; body (DM Mono, 14px, weight 400) for supporting description text
Content max-width 1280px with 24px margin; modal is centered within this layout grid
Motion: modal entrance uses normal duration (300ms) with default easing cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); fade transition for overlay
referencePatterns: Destructive confirmation dialog pattern — two-button layout with danger primary and neutral secondary
Overlay modal panel pattern consistent with ExpenseDetailPopover elevation and shadow treatment from dashboard
Icon + title + body copy layout for contextual warning messaging (trash/warning icon above heading)
Ghost/outline secondary button pattern matching existing chip and border-default token usage across add-expense and dashboard screens
Focus trap pattern within modal — keyboard focus cycles only between X button, Cancel, and Confirm Delete
Dismiss-on-backdrop-click pattern consistent with SettingsDialog drawer dismiss behavior
accessibilityRequirements: WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast ratio of 4.5:1 minimum for all text — silver-text (#CBD5E1) on navy-raised (#293548) meets this requirement
coral-error (#F87171) used as button background must pair with deep-navy (#0F172A) text to achieve sufficient contrast ratio
Modal must use role='dialog' with aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby referencing the modal title element
Modal title must have a unique id referenced by aria-labelledby on the dialog element
Supporting description text must be referenced via aria-describedby on the dialog element
Focus must be trapped within the modal while open; focus returns to the triggering element (delete action in ExpenseDetailPopover) on close
Initial focus on open must land on the Cancel button (safe default) to prevent accidental destructive action
Escape key must dismiss the modal and return to dashboard, equivalent to clicking Cancel
X close button must have aria-label='Close confirmation dialog' for screen reader clarity
Confirm Delete button must have aria-label='Confirm delete expense' to provide full context beyond visual label
Cancel button must have aria-label='Cancel delete and return to dashboard'
Destructive action must not be the default focused element to prevent accidental keyboard activation
Overlay backdrop must have aria-hidden='true' so screen readers do not announce the dimmed background content
All interactive elements meet minimum 44x44px touch target size per design token touch_targets specification
Focus ring uses gold-amber (#F59E0B) with 2px width and 2px offset per state.focus_ring token for visibility on dark surfaces
dataModelDependencies: Expense entity — id field required to identify which expense record is being deleted via DELETE /api/expenses/:id endpoint
Expense entity — merchant and amount fields used to display contextual expense details within the confirmation message (e.g., 'Delete $42.00 at Starbucks?')
Expense entity — date field optionally surfaced in confirmation body copy for additional context
CategoryAggregate entity — indirectly affected post-deletion as DELETE /api/expenses/:id recalculates category aggregates for the month
DailyAggregate entity — indirectly affected post-deletion as daily aggregates are recalculated upon expense deletion
